queues.purgeConfirmDetail: Drop every message in "{{name}}". This cannot be undone.

messages.title: Peek messages in "{{name}}"
messages.requeueOffWarning: Requeue is OFF: fetched messages are dropped (sent to DLX if configured, otherwise lost).

publish.routingKeyPlaceholder: queue name when using default exchange
publish.hint: Sent via lapin with publisher confirms — failure surfaces as an error here.

usage.title: Getting started
usage.step1: Click “Add connection”, fill in host / port / credentials. Defaults match a vanilla Docker `rabbitmq:management` image (guest/guest, ports 5672/15672).
usage.step2: Use “Test connection” — it calls /api/overview via the management plugin (port 15672) to confirm credentials and that the management plugin is enabled.
usage.step3: Switch vhost on the top bar to scope topology lookups; the “(all)” option queries the unfiltered endpoints.
usage.step4: Peek pulls messages with basic.get + reject(requeue=true); your broker copy is unchanged. Turn requeue OFF if you intentionally want to drain.
usage.step5: Publish uses lapin with publisher confirms. The default AMQP exchange routes by queue name in the routing key.
usage.note: lapin handles AMQP framing; topology + purge/delete go through the RabbitMQ Management HTTP API.
